role_title: Associate Manager, New Verticals Strategy & Operations - Merchant Finance

level_signals: Title is Associate Manager (IC contributor, not people manager based on JD context)
Comp range $105,400–$155,000 base consistent with mid-senior IC at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5-10 years of experience in strategy and operations, business operations, consulting, product operations, or similar analytical roles.
You are comfortable working with financial concepts like pricing, fees, discounts, taxes, and payouts, and partnering closely with finance and tax teams.
You can structure ambiguous merchant or payment problems, work across teams to find root causes, and drive a clear plan to fix them (or use AI to fix the issue yourself).
You can work with data (for example in spreadsheets or SQL) to size problems, build simple models, and track performance of your work.
You communicate clearly in writing and in meetings, and you can explain complex topics in simple, direct language to different audiences.
You have experience leading and influencing multiple teams and senior leadership through complex, highly-cross-functional projects.

scope.surface: Merchant Finance systems and processes for Enterprise New Verticals merchants (fees, billing, payouts, tax); Merchant Finance Product Roadmap
scope.business_model: B2B (enterprise merchant-facing operations); marketplace / delivery platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Billing and payout accuracy for enterprise merchants
Reduction in payment, invoicing, and tax issues
Merchant satisfaction / clarity on financial flows
Success metrics for merchant-facing product launches
core_responsibilities: Lead key workflows for merchant fees, billing, payouts, and tax application for U.S. Enterprise New Verticals merchants
Own the Merchant Finance Product Roadmap as Product Operations partner
Design and improve processes to triage and resolve payment, invoicing, and tax issues
Support product builds from idea to launch with business cases, success metrics, and rollout plans
Create and maintain internal documentation and communications for Partner Managers, Accounting, and Support teams

green_flags: Clear ownership scope defined (merchant fees, billing, payouts, tax)
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role has direct path to product roadmap ownership
Applications accepted on an ongoing basis (active hiring)
red_flags: Location type is unclear — JD does not specify city, remote, or hybrid policy despite NYC AEDT notice suggesting possible NYC relevance
No explicit visa sponsorship information provided
Years range '5-10 years' is unusually wide, making seniority expectations ambiguous
No explicit indication of people management, but 'Associate Manager' title may create ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp range, and reporting structure)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false from JD context; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and merchant-facing context)

ambiguities: Location not specified in the JD body. NYC AEDT notice is included, which may suggest NYC office association or remote eligibility for NYC residents, but role location is not confirmed. Treated as 'unclear'.
Years range '5-10 years' is explicitly stated; lower bound (5) used as years_required_min and upper bound (10) as years_required_max.
